title: Cheapest Way to Buy Royal Canin in Canada 2026: 6 Retailers Compared
description: Compare 6 Canadian retailers for Royal Canin in 2026. Chewy Canada Autoship saves up to 35%—find the cheapest price on every formula, including Vet Diet.
verdict: Chewy Canada Autoship delivers the lowest ongoing price for most Royal Canin buyers, combining a 35% first-order discount with 5% recurring savings across the full formula catalog.
content:
Royal Canin occupies a specific position in the Canadian pet food market that makes price shopping both important and complicated. It is one of the handful of brands that veterinary nutritionists genuinely recommend — WSAVA includes Royal Canin alongside Purina Pro Plan and Hill's Science Diet as companies that meet their criteria for nutritional research investment and feeding trial rigor. When my clients' pets are on Royal Canin for a medical reason, or have a strong palatability preference for a specific formula, I am not going to tell them to switch just to save money. What I will tell them is: you are almost certainly overpaying for the same bag by buying it at the wrong retailer.

I tracked Royal Canin pricing across six major Canadian channels over six months with my two golden retrievers — Hugo, 4, and Remy, 2 — and my senior beagle Mochi, 11, as ongoing testers. The same reference products were tracked across each platform: Royal Canin Size Health Nutrition Medium Adult 13.6 kg dry food, Royal Canin Indoor Adult 3.18 kg cat food, and Royal Canin Gastrointestinal Veterinary Diet 7.98 kg. The price gap between the most and least expensive source for the same product hit CAD $30 on large bags during our tracking period.

That is enough money to buy an extra bag every few purchase cycles if you shop strategically. Here is exactly where to find it.

---

## Quick Verdict

**Best ongoing savings — Chewy Canada**: The 35% first-Autoship discount and 5% recurring savings make this the lowest annual cost for most repeat buyers. Full formula catalog, self-serve cancellation, and no subscription lock-in.

**Best for emergencies and Prime members — Amazon Canada**: Next-day delivery in major cities; Subscribe & Save adds 5–10% off. Specialty formula inventory is unreliable.

**Best for in-store buyers — PetSmart Canada**: Higher everyday price, but seasonal sale events hit 20–25% off. Treats loyalty points accumulate toward future buys.

**Best budget price on standard SKUs — Walmart Canada**: Lowest everyday base price, zero setup friction, but only stocks 15–20 Royal Canin formulas versus 60+ elsewhere.

**Only legal source for Veterinary Diet formulas — Your vet's office**: Royal Canin Gastrointestinal, Renal, Hydrolyzed Protein, Urinary SO, and other VD lines are prescription-only in Canada. No retailer can sell them without veterinary authorization.

---

## Testing Methodology

I evaluated each retailer over a six-month period spanning late 2025 and early 2026, purchasing three reference products from each channel: Royal Canin Size Health Nutrition Medium Adult 13.6 kg, Royal Canin Indoor Adult 3.18 kg, and Royal Canin Gastrointestinal Veterinary Diet 7.98 kg (the VD formula obtained through a veterinary partner, since retail channels cannot carry it). I tracked base price, subscription or loyalty discounts, actual shipping costs to a Toronto postal code, account management friction, and formula availability versus Royal Canin Canada's full published catalog. Where retailers did not carry certain formulas, I confirmed this with staff and cross-referenced the Royal Canin Canada website.

---

## What You Are Actually Buying: Royal Canin Ingredient and Nutritional Profile

Before drilling into who charges what, it is worth knowing what separates Royal Canin from other brands at similar price points. That context tells you whether the discount hunting is worth the effort — or whether you should be evaluating alternatives entirely.

Royal Canin's retail dry food lines lead with **chicken meal** as the primary protein in most formulas. That is a meaningful distinction from competitors who list fresh "chicken" first. Fresh chicken is approximately 70% water, which evaporates during cooking and causes the ingredient to drop significantly in ranking post-processing. Chicken meal is rendered, moisture-removed protein that delivers more protein per gram and maintains its ingredient ranking honestly. I have seen clients distrust "meal" ingredients because of marketing narratives that favor fresh-sounding words, but in this context, chicken meal is the more transparent formulation choice.

The approximate guaranteed analysis for Royal Canin Size Health Nutrition Medium Adult — verify against the current bag before purchasing, as formulations can be updated:

- **Crude protein**: minimum 25% (approximately 28% on a dry matter basis after accounting for the 10% moisture cap)
- **Crude fat**: minimum 13% (approximately 14.5% dry matter)
- **Crude fiber**: maximum 4.0%
- **Moisture**: maximum 10%
- **Estimated carbohydrate (by difference)**: approximately 44–48%

The first five ingredients typically include: chicken meal, brown rice, chicken fat, natural chicken flavor, and brewers rice. The rice-forward carbohydrate profile keeps this formula firmly in grain-inclusive territory. That matters because the FDA's 2019 investigation into dilated cardiomyopathy focused specifically on grain-free formulas with elevated legume content — pea protein, pea fiber, lentils — not grain-inclusive products. Royal Canin's conventional retail lines have not been implicated in that ongoing inquiry, which remains unresolved but which most veterinary cardiologists still cite when recommending caution around legume-heavy grain-free diets, particularly in DCM-predisposed breeds like Dobermans and Golden Retrievers.

Royal Canin achieves AAFCO nutritional adequacy via **feeding trials** for most of their core lines — not just formulation calculations. That is the more rigorous pathway. A formulation-only adequacy claim means someone calculated that the recipe should meet AAFCO minimum thresholds in theory. A feeding trial means actual dogs or cats were fed the food under controlled conditions and passed those thresholds in practice. The investment in feeding trial data is a primary reason WSAVA consistently includes Royal Canin on its short recommended list alongside Purina Pro Plan and Hill's Science Diet.

Calorie content for Medium Adult is approximately 3,564 kcal/kg (362 kcal per 100g). For a 28 lb dog consuming roughly 215g per day per feeding guidelines, that is approximately 777 kcal/day — appropriate for a moderately active medium-breed adult. At Chewy Canada Autoship pricing, daily feeding cost for a dog in that range runs approximately CAD $1.40–$1.50/day. Compared to fresh food subscriptions — The Farmer's Dog averages approximately $6.48/day CAD for a similarly sized dog — Royal Canin is a significant value, despite its premium position within dry kibble.

If you are still deciding whether Royal Canin is the right food before deciding where to buy it, see the full [Best Dog Food 2026](/best-dog-food-2026-vet-tested-and-ranked) and [Best Cat Food 2026](/best-cat-food-2026-complete-buying-guide) rankings for broader brand comparisons. If your cat is a senior or managing kidney concerns, [Best Supplements for Senior Cats 2026](/best-supplements-senior-cats-2026-kidney-joint-cognitive) covers what to stack alongside Royal Canin's senior formulas — phosphorus content varies significantly between their senior lines.

---

## Pricing Head-to-Head: 6 Canadian Retailers

All prices in CAD. Pricing tracked through April 2026 — verify before purchasing, as Royal Canin pricing increased approximately 8% across the product line between January 2025 and early 2026, driven in part by ingredient and packaging cost pressures.

| Retailer | RC Medium Adult 13.6 kg | RC Indoor Cat 3.18 kg | Best Discount | Free Shipping | Rating |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Chewy Canada** | $84–$92 (Autoship) | $27–$31 (Autoship) | 35% first order, 5% ongoing | $49 CAD | 9.1/10 |
| **Amazon Canada** | $88–$105 (Sub & Save) | $29–$35 (Sub & Save) | 5–10% Subscribe & Save | $35 CAD (Prime) | 7.8/10 |
| **PetSmart Canada** | $95–$110 (base) | $32–$38 (base) | 5% Treats; 15–25% sale events | $49 CAD | 7.2/10 |
| **Pet Valu** | $92–$108 (base) | $30–$36 (base) | 5% loyalty on Royal Canin | In-store free | 6.8/10 |
| **Walmart Canada** | $85–$98 (base) | $26–$32 (base) | No subscription available | $35 CAD | 6.4/10 |
| **Vet Clinic (VD only)** | N/A | N/A | $110–$140 (Rx formulas only) | N/A | N/A |

---

## Retailer-by-Retailer Breakdown

### 1. Chewy Canada — Rating: 9.1/10

**Best for**: Repeat buyers on any Royal Canin formula who want the lowest annual cost with the least friction

Chewy launched in Canada with the Autoship model intact, and for Royal Canin buyers specifically, it is the most economically rational ongoing purchase channel. The 35% first-Autoship discount is the best new-customer deal available from any Canadian retailer — on a $95 bag, that is CAD $33 back on day one. After that, the 5% ongoing Autoship discount applies to every subsequent delivery.

The catalog depth is what actually seals this recommendation over Amazon. Chewy Canada stocks the full Royal Canin range including breed-specific formulas — French Bulldog, German Shepherd, Golden Retriever, Labrador Retriever lines — life-stage variations, and indoor-specific formulas. Amazon.ca regularly runs out of these specialty SKUs and routes buyers to third-party sellers at 10–15% higher prices who do not participate in Subscribe & Save.

[Check Royal Canin Medium Adult on Amazon](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B00P3MP58M?tag=petverdict-20) to compare Amazon's current price, then benchmark it against Chewy Canada's Autoship rate for the same SKU.

Cancellation is self-serve via the account dashboard — takes approximately 45 seconds with no phone call required and no retention prompts. This matters more than it sounds: the FTC received approximately 70 consumer complaints per day in 2024 about subscription cancellation friction, and pet food subscriptions are a notable category in those complaints. Chewy's model here is genuinely clean by industry standards.

Customer service for Canadian accounts has been a weak point in my experience — response times run 48–72 hours compared to near-instant chat resolution for their US operation. Rural postal codes outside major metros see delivery windows of 5–7 business days, which matters for owners cutting it close on an empty bag.

**Pros**:
- Lowest ongoing price after the first-order discount across the full formula catalog
- 35% first-Autoship discount is the strongest new-customer deal from any Canadian retailer
- Full Royal Canin range including breed-specific and life-stage formulas
- Self-serve cancellation without calling anyone
- Free shipping over CAD $49 covers nearly all Royal Canin bag sizes

**Cons**:
- Canadian customer service response times (48–72 hours) lag behind their US operations
- Rural postal codes see 5–7 business day delivery windows
- No in-store pickup option — all orders require delivery

---

### 2. Amazon Canada — Rating: 7.8/10

**Best for**: Prime members in major cities who need fast delivery and are not locked into a single formula

Amazon.ca's Subscribe & Save discount on Royal Canin ranges from 5% to 10% depending on how many Subscribe & Save items you have active in the same delivery month. With 5+ items scheduled together, the discount bumps to 10% across all subscriptions — competitive with Chewy Autoship in favorable months.

The Prime delivery advantage is real for emergency situations. Next-day delivery on Royal Canin is available in Vancouver, Calgary, Edmonton, Toronto, Ottawa, and Montreal for Prime members. When Mochi ran out of her food mid-week and my next Chewy shipment was three days out, Amazon Prime was the practical bridge.

[Check Royal Canin Indoor Cat on Amazon](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B001P5UB7A?tag=petverdict-20) to see current Prime pricing for cat formulas.

The substantive weakness is inventory reliability. Royal Canin breed-specific and life-stage formulas frequently go out of stock on Amazon.ca's first-party listings and get replaced by third-party sellers who do not participate in Subscribe & Save and charge 10–15% more. Over my six-month tracking period, Royal Canin Indoor Kitten was unavailable on first-party Amazon.ca for approximately three weeks during one stretch. Pricing on the same SKU also fluctuates by CAD $10–15 within a single week — price-tracking browser extensions help capture dips but add friction to what should be a simple recurring purchase.

**Pros**:
- Prime next-day delivery in major Canadian cities — genuinely fast for refills
- Subscribe & Save reaches 10% when bundled with other subscriptions
- Easy to pause or cancel individual Subscribe & Save items
- No platform fee if you already have Prime for other reasons

**Cons**:
- Breed-specific and specialty Royal Canin formulas go out of stock frequently
- Third-party sellers appear at higher prices when first-party inventory runs dry
- Pricing fluctuates by CAD $10–15 on the same SKU week-over-week
- No cold-chain guarantee for products requiring refrigeration (relevant to Azodyl if purchased alongside food)

---

### 3. PetSmart Canada — Rating: 7.2/10

**Best for**: In-store shoppers who can time large purchases around seasonal sale events

PetSmart Canada's everyday price is the highest of the five non-prescription retailers tested — CAD $95–$110 for the 13.6 kg Medium Adult bag. That is a CAD $10–$20 premium over Chewy Canada's Autoship price for the same product. On a day-to-day basis, this is difficult to justify.

Where PetSmart earns its place is the sale calendar. They run Royal Canin-specific promotions two to four times per year, typically reaching 15–25% off the base price. Black Friday discounts in November have historically been the deepest. Stacking a 20% sale event with accumulated Treats loyalty points — earned from prior purchases at 5% back — can bring PetSmart's effective price within CAD $5–10 of Chewy's Autoship rate, close enough to make a two-bag bulk purchase worthwhile.

PetSmart also stocks Royal Canin wet food pouches and canned formulas better than most online retailers. If you are running a mixed-feeding protocol that combines Royal Canin dry with a wet topper — which I often recommend for cats with low thirst drive or for dogs recovering from GI episodes — PetSmart's physical format availability is a practical advantage.

The in-store experience also lets you check lot numbers before purchase, which matters during recall periods. Checking lot numbers against FDA or Canadian Food Inspection Agency recall notices is considerably easier when you have the bag in hand.

**Pros**:
- Physical stores in most Canadian cities for immediate pickup
- Seasonal sales events reach 20–25% off Royal Canin
- Best wet food format availability of the retail channels tested
- In-store staff can help confirm formula compatibility before a large-bag commitment
- Treats loyalty points accumulate toward future purchases

**Cons**:
- Highest everyday base price of the five retail channels
- 5% Treats rebate is lower than Chewy Autoship or Amazon Subscribe & Save
- No first-order discount mechanism comparable to Chewy's 35% welcome deal
- Mobile app for managing Treats rewards is functional but clunky

---

### 4. Pet Valu — Rating: 6.8/10

**Best for**: Canadian-ownership preference, smaller markets, and owners who need Veterinary Diet authorization facilitated in-store

Pet Valu is the dominant Canadian-owned pet specialty chain, with approximately 700 stores across the country. Their Royal Canin pricing sits in the mid-range — CAD $92–$108 for the 13.6 kg dog food line — with a loyalty program offering 5% back on Royal Canin purchases (and 10% on Pet Valu's own in-house brands).

What Pet Valu does distinctly better than the online retailers is physical access to Royal Canin Veterinary Diet formulas. Some Pet Valu locations accept veterinary authorization on file and carry VD formulas for clients to purchase directly in-store — something no online retailer currently handles at scale in Canada. If your veterinarian is comfortable with in-store authorization and there is a Pet Valu nearby, this arrangement can eliminate the intermediary step of ordering through your vet's own dispensary.

Pet Valu also operates a "Nutrition Centre" consultation service at select locations — free 15-minute appointments with staff trained in pet nutrition. In my experience talking with colleagues who have used these consultations, quality varies significantly by location. At its best, it provides useful guidance on formula transitions and multi-pet households. At its worst, it is retail staff reciting bag copy. Worth trying once, but verify any nutritional advice against your veterinarian's guidance before changing a therapeutic diet.

Pet Valu's online experience is a notable weakness. Their website search is slow, product pages lack the nutritional detail found on chewy.ca, and the subscription system has more setup friction than any of the other online platforms I evaluated. For in-store shopping in cities and smaller markets where Chewy or Amazon deliver slowly, Pet Valu is worth the slightly higher price. For purely online purchasing, the case is weaker.

**Pros**:
- Canadian-owned with stores in markets larger retailers do not reach
- Some locations facilitate Royal Canin Veterinary Diet authorization in-store
- 5% loyalty rebate on Royal Canin with no expiration on accumulated points
- Free in-person nutrition consultations at select locations

**Cons**:
- Online experience significantly behind Chewy and Amazon — slow search, limited nutritional details
- Shipping costs and delivery times vary more by region than competitors
- Loyalty savings are lower than Chewy Autoship for most purchase frequencies
- VD formula authorization process varies by location — call ahead before making the trip

---

### 5. Walmart Canada — Rating: 6.4/10

**Best for**: Budget buyers on standard Royal Canin formulas who want the lowest everyday price with zero setup friction

Walmart Canada consistently offers the lowest base price on the Royal Canin formulas they carry — CAD $85–$98 for the 13.6 kg Medium Adult range, CAD $26–$32 for Indoor Cat 3.18 kg. No loyalty sign-up, no subscription configuration, no annual membership. For a buyer who needs only the most common Royal Canin retail SKUs, this is the simplest path to the lowest everyday price.

The selection cap is the dealbreaker for specialty formula buyers. Walmart Canada stocks approximately 15–20 Royal Canin SKUs versus 60+ at Chewy Canada. Breed-specific formulas, life-stage variations beyond basic adult and indoor, and any formula with veterinary adjacency are generally unavailable at Walmart. For Royal Canin French Bulldog Adult, German Shepherd Junior, Maxi Starter, or Indoor Kitten, Walmart simply does not carry them.

Walmart's product pages also lack nutritional detail — no guaranteed analysis, no ingredient list visible without additional research. This is not a dealbreaker for buyers who already know their formula well. But for anyone evaluating a Royal Canin formula for the first time, the inability to check the ingredient order or guaranteed analysis before purchase creates avoidable uncertainty. I would never recommend buying a pet food formula without reading the first five ingredients and verifying the guaranteed analysis, and Walmart's platform makes that unnecessarily difficult.

**Pros**:
- Lowest everyday base price for standard Royal Canin formulas among the retailers tested
- Zero setup friction — no account, subscription, or loyalty card required
- In-store pickup available for online orders
- Free shipping at CAD $35 threshold

**Cons**:
- Only 15–20 Royal Canin SKUs — breed-specific and specialty formulas not stocked
- Product pages lack guaranteed analysis, ingredient lists, and nutritional detail
- No subscription discount mechanism
- No loyalty or first-order savings of any kind

---

### 6. Your Veterinarian's Office — Prescription-Only Formulas

**The only legal source for**: Royal Canin Veterinary Diet formulas (Gastrointestinal, Renal Support, Hydrolyzed Protein HP, Urinary SO, Early Cardiac, Satiety Weight Management, and related Rx lines)

This is not a price comparison discussion — it is a regulatory one. Royal Canin Veterinary Diet formulas are classified as therapeutic foods in Canada and require a veterinary prescription. No retail channel can legally sell them without authorization, and any grey-market online source offering them without a prescription is operating outside Canadian regulatory requirements.

Royal Canin VD formulas typically run CAD $110–$140 for a 7–8 kg bag, versus CAD $90–$120 for a similarly sized retail formula. The price reflects clinical research investment — this is not premium branding on standard kibble. Royal Canin Gastrointestinal High Energy, for example, has published feeding trial data in dogs with exocrine pancreatic insufficiency. When Mochi went through a GI episode last year and was managed on Royal Canin GI Low Fat, the formula specificity was relevant to the clinical outcome, and no amount of price comparison would have made it appropriate to substitute a retail formula.

Some Canadian veterinary clinics now partner with VetSource for home delivery of Rx formulas, allowing clients to order from home with their prescription on file at the clinic. Chewy Canada's Vet Authorizations program — which allows veterinarians to electronically authorize prescriptions for Chewy to fulfill — is in limited Canadian rollout as of April 2026. Worth asking your clinic whether they are enrolled.

**Pros**:
- Only legal source for Royal Canin Veterinary Diet formulas in Canada
- Direct integration with veterinary oversight of the therapeutic protocol
- Some clinics now offer home delivery via VetSource or similar platforms

**Cons**:
- Highest per-bag price of any channel — no subscription discounts at most clinics
- Inconvenient for high-consumption protocols (a large dog on daily Renal Support is an expensive ongoing commitment)
- Clinic hours and stock availability create friction compared to online ordering

---

## Annual Cost Comparison

For a 30 lb adult dog consuming approximately 215g of Royal Canin Size Health Nutrition Medium Adult per day — per their published feeding guidelines for that weight range — one 13.6 kg bag lasts roughly 63 days, or approximately six bags per year.

| Retailer | Per-bag cost (best discount) | Annual cost (6 bags) | Daily cost |
|---|---|---|---|
| Chewy Canada (Autoship) | ~$88 | ~$528 | ~$1.40 |
| Amazon Canada (5% Sub & Save) | ~$93 | ~$558 | ~$1.48 |
| Walmart Canada (base) | ~$91 | ~$546 | ~$1.45 |
| Pet Valu (5% loyalty applied) | ~$98 | ~$588 | ~$1.57 |
| PetSmart Canada (everyday) | ~$102 | ~$612 | ~$1.63 |

First-year Chewy Canada cost is significantly lower due to the 35% discount on the initial bag — approximately CAD $33 off a $95 bag, reducing effective year-one spend toward $495 for a single medium-breed dog. Over three years, the compounding gap between Chewy Autoship and PetSmart everyday pricing for the same product exceeds CAD $250 — enough to fund a complete veterinary dental cleaning, a full senior bloodwork panel, or roughly two additional months of food.

For context on where Royal Canin sits relative to fresh food alternatives: The Farmer's Dog averages approximately CAD $194/month ($6.48/day) for an average-sized adult dog. Even the most expensive Royal Canin retail channel (PetSmart everyday at $1.63/day) is roughly one-quarter the daily cost of fresh-cooked subscription food. Whether that premium for fresh food is clinically justified for a healthy dog is a separate question — for dogs with specific health conditions or established preferences for Royal Canin formulas, the savings relative to fresh food are substantial.

---

## Where Each Retailer Shines

**Chewy Canada** earns its position through the combination of first-order depth (35% off) and ongoing savings (5% Autoship) across a catalog that actually stocks the formula your pet needs. Self-serve cancellation without a phone call is table stakes for a subscription service, and Chewy delivers it while most competitors still require human intervention to cancel.

**Amazon Canada** is the right emergency channel for Prime members in major cities. Next-day delivery on a Wednesday when you just realized the bag is empty is a genuine service, and Subscribe & Save is the simplest possible discount setup for buyers who want to set it and genuinely forget it.

**PetSmart Canada** rewards patience and planning. A November Black Friday purchase of two large bags at 20–25% off, stacked with accumulated Treats points, can get the effective price close to Chewy Autoship for that purchase window. The wet food stocking advantage makes it the best physical retail option for mixed-feeding protocols.

**Pet Valu** serves the customer the big platforms cannot: the rural buyer, the owner who needs to talk to a human about formula compatibility, and the cat or dog on a VD formula where in-store authorization assistance changes the logistics entirely.

**Walmart Canada** is the right answer for one specific scenario: a buyer who needs a standard Royal Canin retail formula with no subscription commitment and wants to pay the lowest possible one-time price. Everything beyond that narrow use case, another channel wins.

---

## Use Case Recommendations

**Starting fresh on Royal Canin**: Order the first bag through Chewy Canada to capture the 35% first-Autoship discount. Set the delivery interval to match actual usage — every 8–10 weeks for most medium-breed dogs. Cancel through the dashboard anytime if it does not work out.

**Managing a household with both retail and Rx Royal Canin**: Use Chewy Canada for the retail SKUs, and your vet clinic or VetSource for the prescription formulas. No single platform handles both at competitive pricing. Do not try to consolidate.

**Trying Royal Canin before committing to a schedule**: Amazon Canada Subscribe & Save with the option to pause after the first delivery. One bag to trial the formula, zero subscription friction beyond a click to pause.

**Budget-maximizing on a standard formula**: Compare Walmart Canada's current price against Chewy Canada's Autoship price for the specific SKU before assuming Walmart wins. For the common size and life-stage formulas, Walmart's base price sometimes beats Chewy's Autoship by a few dollars — but not always. Check both.

**Senior dog or cat on Royal Canin with kidney concerns**: Phosphorus content varies significantly between Royal Canin's senior and renal-support formulas. Before choosing which formula to buy (let alone where to buy it), cross-reference with [Best Supplements for Senior Cats 2026](/best-supplements-senior-cats-2026-kidney-joint-cognitive) for context on managing renal-adjacent nutrition in older pets.

**Comparing Royal Canin against Hill's Science Diet in Canada**: Our sister article [Hill's Science Diet Canada: Best Places to Buy Online in 2026](/hills-science-diet-canada-best-places-buy-online-2026) covers the same retailer landscape for Hill's using the same framework. The relative retailer rankings hold — Chewy Canada wins on both — but the Hill's-specific loyalty and prescription access details differ.

**Owners evaluating whether the premium is worth it**: Both Royal Canin and Hill's Science Diet are WSAVA-recommended, invest in veterinary nutritionist staff, and publish feeding trial data. Whether either is the right choice for a healthy dog with no specific needs — versus a well-formulated budget brand at lower cost — is covered in the [Best Dog Food 2026](/best-dog-food-2026-vet-tested-and-ranked) and [Best Cat Food 2026](/best-cat-food-2026-complete-buying-guide) full rankings. Price-compare retailers only after you have confirmed the brand is the right choice.

---

## The Verdict: Where to Buy Royal Canin in Canada

**Chewy Canada wins for most buyers.** The 35% first-order Autoship discount, 5% ongoing savings, full formula catalog, and frictionless account management make it the default choice for anyone buying Royal Canin on a recurring basis. The annual savings over PetSmart everyday pricing for a single medium-breed dog exceed CAD $80–$100. Over three years, the gap passes $250. That is real money.

**Use Amazon Canada as your emergency and backup channel** — particularly if you have Prime for other reasons and live in a major Canadian city. The delivery speed advantage when you run out unexpectedly is genuine; the specialty formula reliability is not.

**Time PetSmart Canada for sale events** rather than using it as a primary channel. Their seasonal discounts are the one occasion where their pricing becomes competitive. Buying two bags during a 20% Black Friday event while your Treats points are high is a sound strategy. Buying a bag on a random Tuesday is not.

**Pet Valu is the right answer** when you are in a smaller market, need human support, or are navigating the VD formula authorization process in-store.

**Walmart Canada is the no-friction lowest-price option** for the 15–20 standard SKUs they carry. Know what you need before you check out.

For Royal Canin Veterinary Diet, there is no choice — your veterinarian's office is the only legal and clinically appropriate source. The price premium there is not a shopping failure. It is the cost of having a board-certified therapeutic protocol behind the food your pet requires.

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Is Royal Canin cheaper at the vet or at a pet store in Canada?

For standard retail Royal Canin formulas — Size Health Nutrition, Breed Health Nutrition, life-stage lines — pet stores and online retailers are consistently cheaper than veterinary clinics. Veterinary offices primarily stock Royal Canin Veterinary Diet formulas, which are prescription-only and priced higher. If your vet is selling you Royal Canin Medium Adult alongside prescription foods, their per-bag price almost certainly exceeds Chewy Canada's Autoship rate for the same product. Compare the bag you are actually buying against Chewy's Autoship price before your next vet visit.

### Can I buy Royal Canin Veterinary Diet without a prescription in Canada?

No. Royal Canin Veterinary Diet formulas — Gastrointestinal, Renal Support, Hydrolyzed Protein, Urinary SO, Early Cardiac, and related lines — require a veterinary prescription in Canada. Any online seller offering them without prescription documentation is operating outside Canadian regulatory requirements. These formulas are designed for specific diagnosed medical conditions and warrant veterinary oversight. Purchasing through unauthorized channels also voids any quality assurance the brand provides, and means no one is monitoring whether the therapeutic diet is achieving its clinical goal.

### Does Royal Canin go on sale in Canada, and when?

Yes. PetSmart Canada runs the most predictable Royal Canin promotions — Black Friday in November, a spring promotion in March–April, and occasional summer events — typically 15–25% off the base price. Amazon Canada pricing fluctuates more frequently but less predictably; price-tracking tools can capture meaningful dips on common SKUs. Chewy Canada's Autoship price stays stable, but periodic promotional emails offering an extra 10% off Autoship orders appear several times a year and stack with the base 5% discount. Subscribe to Chewy's email list for those.

### Is the Royal Canin sold at Walmart Canada the same formula as at PetSmart or Chewy?

Yes — for the specific SKUs Walmart carries, the product is identical to what you would buy anywhere else. Royal Canin's North American supply draws primarily from their Guelph, Ontario manufacturing facility and US production. Formula and manufacturing standards are consistent regardless of retailer. Lot numbers differ by shipment batch, which is expected. If you ever have a recall concern, the lot number on the bag is the relevant identifier, not which retailer sold it.

### Why does Royal Canin cost more than other kibble at a similar AAFCO compliance level?

Several compounding factors: Royal Canin employs more board-certified veterinary nutritionists than most competitors, publishes more peer-reviewed feeding trial research, and manufactures breed-specific and condition-specific formulas that require separate production runs. More SKUs means higher per-unit manufacturing cost. Their palatability research investment is also unusually deep — their formulas are engineered for digestibility and stool quality outcomes, not just nutrient content. Whether this premium over similarly WSAVA-recommended brands like Purina Pro Plan — which is cheaper and also invests in feeding trials — is clinically justified depends on whether your pet has a specific reason to be on Royal Canin. For a healthy dog with no breed predisposition or health condition, Pro Plan's ingredient profile and price point is competitive. For a dog whose GI system specifically responds to Royal Canin's formulation, the premium is often justifiable.

### How much Royal Canin does a medium-sized dog need per month?

For a 30 lb adult dog on Royal Canin Size Health Nutrition Medium Adult, the feeding guideline recommends approximately 210–220g per day. At 215g/day, a 13.6 kg bag lasts roughly 63 days. Budget approximately six bags per year for a dog in that weight range. For a 50 lb dog, daily feeding amounts increase to approximately 290–310g, shortening each bag to around 46 days and requiring approximately eight bags annually. I recommend starting at the lower end of the published range and adjusting based on body condition scoring — feeding guidelines on most Royal Canin bags trend slightly generous, and maintaining an appropriate body condition score matters more than hitting a midpoint number.

### What is Chewy Canada's return policy on Royal Canin if my pet refuses it?

Chewy Canada's return policy for pet food is consistent with their US operation: if your pet does not accept the food or you ordered the wrong formula, they typically offer a refund or replacement without requiring you to ship the product back — particularly on a first purchase of a new formula. This policy has remained consistent in my testing through early 2026. PetSmart Canada accepts unopened food returns within 60 days with a receipt. Amazon Canada returns for grocery and pet food items are handled case-by-case through seller support and are less predictable than the dedicated pet retailers. If you are trialing a Royal Canin formula for the first time with a picky eater, Chewy Canada's return flexibility is a meaningful practical advantage over buying from a retailer with a stricter policy.

Royal Canin Indoor Adult Dry Cat Food
